Sugar over medium-high heat until temperature reaches 105°C on a candy thermometer, stirring
occasionally. Remove from heat and allow to cool for 5 minutes. Skim off any foam with metal spoon.

Add 1 teaspoon vanilla extract and ½ teaspoon almond extract for enhanced flavor. Return to heat
and bring back to rolling boil. Immediately ladle into hot sterilized jars, filling to ½ inch
from rim. Wipe rims with damp cloth and seal with lids.

Process in water bath for 10 minutes, then remove and cool completely on towel. Jars should
seal with distinct popping sound as they cool. Store in cool, dark cupboard for up to 12 months.

Cherry Jam

A classic preserve with bright cherry flavor, perfect for spreading on toast or filling pastries.

Ingredients:
1.5 kg fresh sweet cherries, pitted and halved
750g preserving sugar
Juice of 1 lemon
1 tablespoon kirsch or brandy (optional)
1 vanilla pod, split lengthwise
Pinch of sea salt

Method:
Place pitted cherries in large preserving pan with sugar, lemon juice, and vanilla pod. Stir
over low heat until sugar dissolves completely, approximately 5 minutes. Increase heat to medium-high
and bring to rolling boil, skimming surface of any impurities and foam.

Continue cooking until mixture reaches setting point (105°C on thermometer), approximately 20-25
minutes. Remove from heat and let rest for 5 minutes. Add kirsch if using. Ladle into hot
sterilized jars, leaving ½ cm headspace. Seal immediately with lids and rings.

Cool completely before labeling. The jam will thicken further as it cools. Store in a cool,
dark place.

Yield: About 4-5 jars (300ml each)
Preparation time: 20 minutes
Cooking time: 30 minutes
